message:
docs(artifacts): update RaptorQ contract, closure signoff, Track-E supersession, and Track-F benchmark data

Six artifact and documentation updates from ongoing RaptorQ optimization:

- raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json: Update generated_at
  timestamp and add replay_bundle_well_formed validation command to the
  unit_commands list, ensuring the new test is part of the contract's
  self-validation surface.

- raptorq_program_closure_signoff_packet_v1.json: Update generated_at
  timestamp and add replay_bundle_well_formed test to the G7 replay
  commands list, keeping the signoff packet in sync with the contract.

- raptorq_track_e_gf256_bench_v1.json: Add supersession metadata to the
  2026-03-02 same-session retention decision, marking it as superseded
  by simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04. Add decision_role and supersedes
  fields to the 2026-03-04 canonical decision. Add explanatory finding
  noting the supersession relationship. This formalizes the decision
  chain so automated tooling can trace which ablation result is current.

- raptorq_track_f_wavefront_pipeline_v1.json: Fresh benchmark data from
  latest wavefront pipeline runs across all three scenarios. Updated
  p50/p95/p99 timing values and delta_vs_sequential percentages.

- docs/raptorq_baseline_bench_profile.md: Add section documenting the
  replay bundle validation test and its role in contract integrity.

- docs/raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract.md: Add cross-reference
  to the replay bundle well-formedness test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -237,13 +237,24 @@ Recorded in `artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_bench_v1.json` under `simd_policy_a
 - Large balanced addmul window candidate (`addmul_total=24576..32768`, `addmul_min_lane=12288`) showed no meaningful `addmul_slices2_auto` uplift (`+0.1438%`, `p=0.82`) and regressed `mul_slices2_auto` (`+2.1554%` median).
 - Mul-only window candidate (`mul_total=32768`) regressed `mul_slices2_auto` (`+0.6484%`, `p=0.02`) on the same scenario.
 
+This 2026-03-02 packet is now explicitly a historical comparator, not the
+current default contract. The artifact marks that machine-checkably via
+`simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_02.decision.supersession.status = superseded`
+and
+`simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_02.decision.supersession.superseded_by = simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04`.
+
 Updated decision after broader corpus (`simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04`):
 
 This default-selection result is recorded in
 `artifacts/raptorq_track_e_gf256_bench_v1.json` under
 `simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04.decision` and is the canonical E5 artifact for
 the current x86 auto-window contract.
 
+The artifact now also pins that role directly:
+`simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04.decision.decision_role = canonical_current_x86_default_contract`
+and
+`simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_04.decision.supersedes = ["simd_policy_ablation_2026_03_02"]`.
+
 - Keep `mul` auto window disabled by default on x86 (`mul_min_total > mul_max_total`).
 - Move x86 `addmul` auto window to `24576..32768` total bytes with `addmul_min_lane=8192`.
 - Rationale: same-target deterministic corpus over `RQ-E-GF256-DUAL-*` showed strongest repeatable uplift in balanced high-throughput lanes (`DUAL-004/005/006`), with targeted `addmul_slices2_auto` median deltas of `-6.1424%`, `-14.4411%`, and `-6.3938%` versus baseline (`avg -8.9924%`).

@@ -110,6 +110,10 @@ Primary replay anchor:
 
 - `rch exec -- cargo test --test raptorq_perf_invariants g7_expected_loss_contract_schema_and_coverage -- --nocapture`
 
+Replay-bundle integrity verifier:
+
+- `rch exec -- cargo test --test raptorq_perf_invariants g7_expected_loss_contract_replay_bundle_is_well_formed -- --nocapture`
+
 ## Closure Readiness Contract
 
 The artifact includes a machine-checkable `closure_readiness` section to avoid
